steps.title: Interpret the map comparison
steps.content: Hawthorne compares historians' and biographers' knowledge to a map: it may be accurate and useful, but it is cold and lacks the charm of landscape painting. He suggests strict biographical exactness limits liveliness.

steps.title: Reject literal or invented claims
steps.content: Choice A shifts to fiction writers' skills. Choice B introduces fame, which the passage does not discuss. Choice C takes the map and painting metaphor literally. The remaining choice captures the trade-off between truthfulness and engagement. The answer is D.
